name: understand-onboard
tagline: Generates a project onboarding guide (architecture, tour, complexity hotspots) from a local knowledge-graph JSON built by a companion skill.
verdict: Clean, local-only skill: no network calls, no credential access, no destructive file ops, and it even quotes/validates a git commit hash before use to avoid shell injection. Minor polish gaps only: no explicit negative-trigger guidance, no version/changelog signal, and 'highest complexity' hotspot threshold is left undefined. Safe to install.

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger statement; the prerequisite (`/understand` must have run first) is only surfaced as a runtime failure path (line 33), not in the description, so a user could invoke it nee
No worked example of graph JSON input or sample guide output; 'highest complexity values' (step 7, line 58) has no defined threshold or count, leaving the hotspot selection ambiguous.
No version field or changelog/last-updated marker anywhere in the file, so consumers can't detect drift between installed and upstream copies.
